name: bc
readme:
Short:        GNU arbitrary precision calculator
Type:         util/cli
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       Philip A. Nelson (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.07.1

bc - GNU arbitrary precision calculator

Original author: Philip A. Nelson
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested on FS-UAE with Workbench 3.1 (37/37 tests pass).

bc is an arbitrary precision calculator language supporting variables,
functions, and a C-like syntax. The -l flag loads a math library with
transcendental functions (sin, cos, arctan, ln, exp, bessel).

Usage:
  bc [-hlqswv] [file ...]

  bc                   Interactive calculator
  bc -l                Load math library (e, l, s, c, a, j functions)
  bc -q script.bc      Run script without welcome banner
  bc -c script.bc      Compile to bytecode (don't execute)

Examples:
  echo "2^10" | bc               Result: 1024
  echo "scale=10; 4*a(1)" | bc -l   Pi to 10 places: 3.1415926532

Replaces the ixemul-dependent bc 1.6 on Aminet (dev/gg/bc-1.6)
with a standalone noixemul binary. No runtime library dependencies.

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
porting_notes: GNU bc 1.07.1 is a yacc/lex-generated multi-file port (12 .c files). Key transformations: signal(SIGINT) replaced with amiport_check_break() polling in the execute loop for Ctrl-C support; isatty() replaced with amiport_isatty(); getenv() replaced with amiport_getenv() with tracked cleanup via atexit(); fread()-based YY_INPUT replaces read()/fileno() in the scanner; all bc_exit(1) calls changed to bc_exit(10) for Amiga RETURN_ERROR (8 instances across main.c, scan.c, load.c, util.c); exit(YY_EXIT_FAILURE) changed to bc_exit(20) for RETURN_FAIL; fclose(yyin) guarded against stdin close. Bundled GNU getopt used instead of broken libnix getopt_long (crash-patterns #17). --allow-multiple-definition linker flag needed for bundled getopt vs libnix symbol clash. libmath.h generated via bootstrap. 37/37 FS-UAE tests pass including real-world stress tests: factorial(50), fibonacci(100), 10K loop iterations, pi to 100 digits. Perf-optimizer identified DIVU→shift opportunities and free-list pooling for number.c hot loops (noted, not yet applied).
known_limitations: No readline/libedit line editing in interactive mode. BC_ENV_ARGS only works if set in AmigaOS ENV: variable. random() mapped to rand() — adequate for bc's non-cryptographic use.
